input_diagnostics: candidate item=bunch; complement=animate plural; determiner=a; known CGEL boundary case
registered_prediction: Animate plural complements should favour plural agreement if notional basis supplies the agreement controller.
success_condition: Filtered subject-position opportunities show plural agreement dominant and counter-direction singular hits are rare or explainable by non-target coding.
failure_condition: Surface singular head number predicts the data as well as or better than notional basis, or most apparent plural hits are query artifacts.
notes: Confirmatory target because the earlier pilot found animate plural support but this lane requires denominator and reproducible coding.

input_diagnostics: candidate item=bunch; complement=inanimate plural; determiner=a; known CGEL boundary case
registered_prediction: Inanimate complements should either show a distinct singular/collective profile or expose bunch as an unstable boundary item rather than central AGR evidence.
success_condition: Filtered denominator distinguishes an inanimate profile from the animate profile or records sparse evidence with bounded uncertainty.
failure_condition: The same plural profile appears with no difference from animate complements and no defensible boundary interpretation is possible.
notes: A failure here may improve the graph by forcing bunch out of central agreement evidence.

input_diagnostics: candidate items=majority/minority; complement plural or definite set; borderline number-transparent/collective profile
registered_prediction: Agreement should be mixed or context-conditioned rather than reducible to surface singular head number.
success_condition: Filtered data show non-trivial plural agreement conditioned by complement/set construal, register, or determiner frame.
failure_condition: Agreement is uniformly singular over relevant opportunities or raw plural hits collapse under KWIC filtering.
notes: Use only if query cells can separate majority/minority as quantified-set heads from unrelated uses.

input_diagnostics: candidate item=couple; sense split collective pair vs approximate quantity; complement plural
registered_prediction: The approximate-quantity sense should pattern differently from the collective-pair sense in controller identification and agreement realization.
success_condition: KWIC coding can separate senses and shows different agreement profiles by sense.
failure_condition: Sense separation is too noisy for corpus inference or both senses pattern identically after filtering.
notes: Optional tranche; run only after bunch and majority/minority are coded.

input_diagnostics: candidate items=smattering/gaggle/tranche; animate plural complements; low-frequency or constructionally marginal QN-like frames
registered_prediction: Animate plural-member construal should support plural agreement availability or dominance when the subject frame profiles members rather than group-as-unit, even where item-specific agreement listing is weak.
success_condition: Pre-registered low-frequency cells show filtered plural or context-conditioned agreement consistent with notional/controller diagnostics, not just stored item preference.
failure_condition: Cells are too sparse for inference, collapse to item-specific idiosyncrasy, or require post-hoc listing exceptions that AGR did not predict.
notes: Direct-string COCA run returned zero matches in all six cells; revise query design before interpreting.

input_diagnostics: same low-frequency QN tranche; no independent stored preference assumed before data inspection
registered_prediction: The listing baseline should be uncertain, default to surface-head singular, or require a new item-specific listing once local distribution is observed.
success_condition: The baseline stays live if each item shows idiosyncratic local behaviour or if sparse cells block a generalization test.
failure_condition: The baseline loses pressure if several low-frequency cells generalize in the AGR-predicted direction before item-specific listing is justified.
notes: Direct-string COCA run returned zero matches in all six cells, so the baseline remains live.
